Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to compute total duration lead time for each case from first event Submit Claim to last event Close Claim Then compare durations to see which are long

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases with Request Additional Documents likely add delay especially if multiple iterations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2001 Duration 9 00 to 10 30 equals 1 5 hours Very short

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 Duration from 2024 04 01 09 05 to 2024 04 02 11 00 equals about 25 hours 55 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 Duration from 2024 04 01 09 10 to 2024 04 03 09 30 equals about 48 hours 20 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2004 Duration 9 20 to 10 45 equals 1 hour 25 minutes Very short

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Duration from 2024 04 01 09 25 to 2024 04 04 14 30 equals about 77 hours 05 minutes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Compare durations 2001 about 1 5h 2002 about 25h55 2003 about 48h20 2004 about 1h25 2005 about 77h05 So cases with significantly longer durations are 2002 2003 and 2005

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All three longer cases contain Request Additional Documents while the two short cases do not

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The duration roughly correlates with number of requests 2002 has one request 2003 has two requests 2005 has three requests

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Low complexity cases 2001 and 2004 are short Medium complexity case 2002 is longer High complexity cases 2003 and 2005 are longest

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity seems to be strongly correlated with longer duration because higher complexity cases require more requests for additional documents

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2002 has a large delay from Request Additional Documents at 14 00 to Approve Claim at 10 00 next day about 20 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2003 has multiple long delays especially from second Request Additional Documents at 17 00 to Approve Claim at 16 00 next day about 23 hours and from Approve Claim at 16 00 to Pay Claim next day at 09 00 about 17 hours

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 has repeated long waits between requests about 29 hours 30 minutes then about 22 hours then about 19 hours before approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The big delays likely reflect waiting for customers to provide additional documents and repeated review cycles

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Adjuster Mike handles one low complexity fast case and one high complexity long case Adjuster Lisa handles one low complexity fast case one medium complexity longer case and one high complexity very long case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: There is no strong evidence that a single adjuster alone is the root cause because both adjusters show short low complexity cases and longer higher complexity cases

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region A has one fast low complexity case and one long high complexity case Region B has one fast low complexity case one medium longer case and one very long high complexity case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region alone does not appear to be the main driver because both regions can be fast when complexity is low

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Region B may still contribute to extra delay in difficult claims because the high complexity Region B case is longer than the high complexity Region A case but the sample is too small for a firm conclusion

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Manager Bill appears only in the two high complexity long cases while Manager Ann appears in low and medium cases

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests manager assignment is likely linked to claim complexity rather than being the primary cause of delay

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The primary root cause is the iterative document request loop which is triggered mainly by higher complexity claims and creates long waiting periods

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Possible contributing factors are incomplete initial submissions unclear upfront document requirements slower customer response time for complex claims and possible difficulty by adjusters in identifying all needed documents in the first request

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Suggestions improve upfront document checklists by complexity level triage high complexity claims to experienced adjusters use automated completeness checks and reminders and streamline the approval to payment handoff
